subject: Cloud Computing
title: CDN to Compute: Cloudflare Workers
description: One-minute skill: CDN to Compute: Cloudflare Workers.

hook.text: CDNs used to just cache static files. Now Cloudflare Workers runs your code at 300 locations worldwide in milliseconds.

buildup.text: Traditional serverless runs in one region. Edge functions run at the CDN edge — whichever node is closest to the user.

discovery.text: Workers uses V8 isolates instead of containers. Startup: under 5 milliseconds. Cold starts are nearly eliminated.

twist.text: Edge functions have constraints: limited CPU, smaller memory, no filesystem. You write code differently for the edge.

climax.text: Deno Deploy, Vercel Edge, and Fastly Compute all offer similar models. The edge runtime is becoming a new standard.

punchline.text: Your code runs in 300 cities. Five milliseconds cold start.

quiz.question: How do Cloudflare Workers achieve near-zero cold starts?
options: They use lightweight V8 isolates instead of full containers
They keep every function running permanently at all locations
They predict which functions will be called and pre-load them
